mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate compresses the risk-free rate, lowering equity discount rates and supporting valuations, leading to positive forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a rapid deterioration in the labor market and a recessionary demand collapse, which will depress corporate earnings and equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% within the last 3 months
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE > 5.5%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in payrolls and retail sales together indicate a synchronized collapse in labor income and consumer spending, a powerful negative signal for aggregate demand and forward equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS and RSAFS show a month-over-month decline
failure_condition: If the joint rank correlation (PAYEMS and RSAFS) with 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative, or if more than 50% of triggered instances yield positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a deep recession, a positive inflection in industrial production after a prolonged decline signals the beginning of a supply-side recovery, leading to positive forward returns over 60 trading days; in expansion the same pattern would be a normal fluctuation and not predictive.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO shows a positive month-over-month change after at least two consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during the 2020 recession, the regime-conditional hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below the 1000 thousand-unit level signals a severe contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which precedes a broad economic slowdown and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1000.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable CPI level indicates subdued inflation, which allows the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative monetary policy, reducing uncertainty and supporting equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of CPIAUCSL against 20-day forward returns is less than 0.02 in absolute value over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound and industrial production is simultaneously declining, monetary policy has exhausted conventional easing while the real economy continues to contract, a potent negative signal for forward equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS = 0.09 (near zero) and INDPRO declining month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint signal (INDPRO declining and FEDFUNDS near zero) yields positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-recession regime, when unemployment peaks and begins to decline, it signals the start of a labor market recovery, which is a leading indicator for economic revival and positive forward equity returns over 60 trading days; in normal expansions rising unemployment would be negative.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of triggered instances during the 2020 recession recovery period.
